title: FOODEEG: An open dataset of human electroencephalographic and behavioural responses to food images
description: FOODEEG is an open dataset comprising electroencephalographic (EEG) and behavioral responses from 117 participants to food images. The dataset includes raw and processed EEG recordings from a food categorization task, along with behavioral data from food go/no-go and paired choice tasks. Participants also provided continuous ratings on food attributes and completed questionnaires assessing dietary styles and eating motivations, with normative ratings collected from an additional 624 online participants.
methods_description: Neural and behavioral data were collected across two sessions. Session 1 involved a food categorization task with EEG recording where participants viewed food images for 2 seconds and categorized them on healthiness, tastiness, or willingness to eat, followed by continuous ratings (0-100) on these dimensions. Session 2 included a food go/no-go task and a food paired choice task. Normative ratings on 22 food attributes were collected from online samples using continuous scales (0-100).

affiliations.name: Division of Science, New York University Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates
